title: ACB Stock Down as Q1 Earnings Miss Estimates, Revenues Up Y/Y
text: Aurora Cannabis reported first-quarter fiscal 2026 loss per share from continuing operations of 26 cents, which missed Zacks Consensus Estimate of earnings of 18 cents per share. Shares of company lost nearly 6.1% in pre-market hours following earnings miss announcement. However, company reported bright spot: Total net revenues of $98 million, up 17.5% year over year, beating Zacks Consensus Estimate by 38.3%. Revenues were driven by strong momentum in international medical cannabis markets, with higher sales to Australia, Germany, Poland, and United Kingdom, and increased revenues in Canada supported by insurance-covered and self-paying patients. Despite EPS miss, revenue beat demonstrates underlying strength in core business, particularly in higher-margin international medical cannabis operations. Company's challenge appears to be profitability timing rather than revenue generation capability. Market reaction to earnings miss suggests investors focusing on near-term profitability metrics despite strong top-line growth. Analyst responses may shift as company demonstrates ability to convert revenue growth into improved earnings.
